subject: current-affairs
note: SAMPLE UPSC static-GK questions (stable facts, source 'Sample (UPSC static GK)'). Replace with verified current-affairs PYQs + SME solutions + 13-language translations before shipping. Kept to non-expiring facts to avoid staleness.

prompt.en: Which body is responsible for conducting elections in India?

solution.en: The Election Commission of India (ECI), a permanent constitutional body under Article 324, conducts elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of the President and Vice-President.

prompt.en: In which year was the Reserve Bank of India established?

solution.en: The RBI was established on 1 April 1935 under the RBI Act, 1934. It was nationalised in 1949.

prompt.en: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?

solution.en: The UN headquarters is in New York City, USA. Geneva, Vienna and Nairobi host major UN offices, and UNESCO is headquartered in Paris.

prompt.en: Which body did the NITI Aayog replace in 2015?

solution.en: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) replaced the Planning Commission on 1 January 2015 as the government's premier policy think-tank.

prompt.en: The Fundamental Duties were added to the Constitution by which amendment?

solution.en: The Fundamental Duties (Article 51A) were added by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, on the recommendation of the Swaran Singh Committee.

prompt.en: In which year was the Goods and Services Tax (GST) introduced in India?

solution.en: GST came into effect on 1 July 2017, subsuming most indirect taxes into a single unified tax under the 101st Constitutional Amendment.

prompt.en: Who appoints the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India?

solution.en: The CAG is appointed by the President of India under Article 148 and audits the accounts of the Union and the States.

prompt.en: The international agreement on climate change adopted in 2015 is known as the:

solution.en: The Paris Agreement was adopted at COP21 in 2015 to limit global warming to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels.

prompt.en: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) of India was established in which year?

solution.en: The NHRC was established on 12 October 1993 under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993.

prompt.en: The World Health Organization (WHO) is headquartered in which city?

solution.en: The WHO, a specialised agency of the UN responsible for international public health, is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

prompt.en: In which city is the headquarters of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) located?

solution.en: The IMF, one of the Bretton Woods institutions, is headquartered in Washington, D.C., close to the World Bank.

prompt.en: Which organisation compiles and maintains the 'Red List' of Threatened Species?

choices.en: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)
Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species (CITES)

solution.en: The IUCN Red List is the world's most comprehensive inventory of the global conservation status of species, classifying them from 'Least Concern' to 'Extinct'.

prompt.en: The Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation of which type of ecosystem?

solution.en: Signed in Ramsar, Iran, the convention provides the framework for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.

prompt.en: How many goals are there in the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)?

solution.en: The SDGs, adopted in 2015 as successors to the eight Millennium Development Goals, comprise 17 goals covering issues from poverty to climate action.

prompt.en: The Montreal Protocol is an international agreement aimed primarily at:

solution.en: The Montreal Protocol phases out the production of ozone-depleting substances such as chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) to protect the stratospheric ozone layer.

prompt.en: Which statutory body is responsible for regulating the securities (share) markets in India?

choices.en: Securities and Exchange Board of India

solution.en: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) is the regulator for the securities and commodity markets, protecting investor interests.

prompt.en: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is chiefly associated with which of the following?

solution.en: The 73rd Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions, adding Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule; the 74th dealt with municipalities.

prompt.en: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?

solution.en: Under Article 89, the Vice President of India is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha and presides over its sittings.

prompt.en: The headquarters of the World Trade Organization (WTO) is located in which city?

solution.en: The WTO, which regulates international trade among member nations, is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

prompt.en: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the Finance Commission constituted?

solution.en: Article 280 provides for a Finance Commission, constituted by the President every fifth year, to recommend the distribution of taxes between the Union and States.

prompt.en: The Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions relating to:

choices.en: Allocation of Rajya Sabha seats to States

solution.en: The Tenth Schedule, added by the 52nd Amendment (1985), lays down the anti-defection law disqualifying members who defect from their party.

prompt.en: The Human Development Index (HDI) is published annually by which organisation?

solution.en: The UNDP publishes the HDI in its Human Development Report, combining indicators of life expectancy, education, and per-capita income.

prompt.en: In which city is the headquarters of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) located?

solution.en: ISRO is headquartered in Bengaluru, Karnataka; Sriharikota hosts the launch site, while Thiruvananthapuram houses the VSSC rocket centre.

prompt.en: In India, the 'repo rate' is determined by which of the following?

choices.en: Securities and Exchange Board of India

solution.en: The repo rate, at which the central bank lends to commercial banks, is set by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee as a key monetary policy tool.

prompt.en: The Kyoto Protocol is primarily concerned with which of the following?

choices.en: Control of transboundary movement of hazardous wastes

solution.en: The Kyoto Protocol, adopted under the UNFCCC, committed industrialised nations to binding targets for reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

prompt.en: In the context of the Union Budget, the term 'fiscal deficit' is best defined as:

choices.en: The excess of revenue expenditure over revenue receipts
The difference between a country's exports and imports
The excess of total expenditure over total receipts excluding borrowings
The excess of total expenditure over total receipts including borrowings

solution.en: Fiscal deficit is total government expenditure minus total receipts excluding borrowings; it indicates the total borrowing the government needs to meet its expenditure.
